title: inula orientalis subsp. grandiflora
intro: Inula orientalis subsp. grandiflora is a subspecies of the Inula orientalis plant, commonly known as the Oriental elecampane or giant elecampane, a perennial herb native to Asia and Europe. This plant has been used for centuries in traditional medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiviral properties. The health benefits of Inula orientalis subsp. grandiflora include relieving respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as treating digestive problems and skin conditions. Inula orientalis subsp. grandiflora can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, as well as being used in essential oils and topical creams for its medicinal benefits.
